title: Accessible Player Experiences
creator: AbleGamers
description: A design-pattern framework organized around the experiences players need, such as controlling input, receiving information, understanding state, and recovering from mistakes. Patterns link barriers to possible design solutions.
useful: Starting from player experience keeps accessibility connected to design intent instead of treating it as a list of special options.
audience: Designers and user researchers shaping features before or during implementation.
learning: Frame accessibility around desired player experiences
Match recurring barriers with design patterns
Discuss solutions without assuming one feature fits everyone
